data.title: EQT AB Non-GAAP EPS of €1.12, revenue of €2.73B
data.description:
• EQT AB (publ) press release (EQBBF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €1.12.
  • Revenue of €2.73B (+3.0% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA amounted to €1,642 m ( €1,359 m), corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 60 % (58%).
  • The Board proposes a dividend to the shareholders of SEK 5.00 (4.30) per share for the fiscal year 2025. The dividend is proposed to be paid out in two installments, SEK 2.50 (2.15) in May 2026 and SEK 2.50 (2.15) in December 2026.
  • Gross inflows amounted to € 26bn – more than double the € 11bn of inflows in 2024 . FAUM increased to € 141bn (€ 136bn).
  • Total AUM was € 270bn ( € 269bn) at the end of the period.

data.title: Unit prices upsized senior notes offering of $1B
data.description:
• Uniti Group (UNIT) priced an offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of 8.625% senior notes due 2032.
  • The offering was upsized by $500M aggregate principal amount compared to the previously announced offering size of $500M.
  • Net proceeds will repay borrowings under Uniti Services' senior secured first lien term loan due 2031, covering fees and expenses, with remaining funds for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 4, 2026.

data.title: Bankinter, S.A. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Bankinter, S.A. press release (BKIMF): Q4 Revenue of €569.99M (+3.6% Y/Y).
  • Return on equity stood at a record 18.9%, one percentage point higher than the same ratio last year, with ROTE at 20%.
  • Meanwhile, the CET1 capital ratio ended the period at 12.72%.
  • In terms of efficiency, the institution continued to lead its peers with a cost-toincome ratio of 36.1%, marking a year-on-year improvement of 23 basis points.
  • Looking at asset quality, the NPL ratio fell by 17 basis points compared with 2024, to 1.94%, with a very robust coverage ratio of 68%.

data.title: Resolute Mining reports Q4 results; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Resolute Mining press release (RMGGF): Q4 Group gold production of 65,918 ounces (oz) (Q3 2025: 59,857oz) meeting expectations due to continued stockpile processing at Mako (Senegal) and improved underground output at Syama (Mali).
  • All-In Sustaining Costs of $1,877/oz (Q3 2025: $2,205/oz) in line with expectations as higher gold production partially offset higher royalty payments.
  • Quarterly capital expenditure (excluding exploration) of $18.4 million (Q3 2025: $26.6 million) in line with expectations consisting of $16.0 million non-sustaining and $2.4 million of sustaining capital.
  • Q4 operating cash flow generation of $85.7 million (Q3: $67.8 million) (operating cash flow before capital expenditure, exploration and working capital).

data.title: Thailand’s Gulf Development teams up with Google to explore AI opportunities
data.description:
• Billionaire Sarath Ratanavadi’s Gulf Development Pcl, Thailand’s top power producer, is expanding into artificial intelligence through a new partnership with Alphabet Inc.’s Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to explore joint business ventures.
  • Gulf Edge Co., Gulf Development’s unit, signed a strategic framework agreement with Google to provide AI infrastructure services, according to a Thai exchange filing.
  • The partnership aims to support Gulf Development’s transition into an AI-native technology company, it said.
  • The collaboration also aims to launch commercial services for enterprise (B2B) and consumer (B2C) engagement by leveraging Google’s Gemini models while simultaneously modernizing business processes through agentic AI.

data.title: CenterPoint Energy boosts workforce to 3,300 ahead of winter storm in Texas
data.description:
• CenterPoint Energy (CNP) said Wednesday it has added more than 600 frontline workers, bringing its total workforce to 3,300, as the company prepares for an approaching winter storm. Texas emergency response resources have also been activated ahead of potential impacts.
  • ERCOT issued a Weather Watch for Saturday through Tuesday, though grid conditions are expected to remain normal.
  • Additionally, CenterPoint Energy (CNP) said it was standing up three staging sites Thursday at strategic locations across the northern portion of the Greater Houston area to pre-position resources to support potential restoration efforts.
  • Source: Press Release
